explanation: This is the original true positive command and is not a bypass. It is included for reference.
The '-o' argument is split into '- o' using space insertion. Some programs may still interpret this as '-o' depending on parsing logic, making it a potential bypass.
The '-o' flag is replaced with '--option=o' using substitution of long-form syntax. This may bypass detection if the Sigma rule does not account for long-form options.
The '-o' flag is followed by an escaped newline character. This uses insertion and encoding techniques to obfuscate the command line while maintaining functionality.
The '-o' flag is followed by a quote, which may be parsed as part of the flag in some contexts. This uses insertion to potentially bypass detection.
The '-o' flag is followed by an escaped single quote. This uses recoding techniques to obfuscate the command line.
